mnemonics: HATS: Remember the four key UX measures with the acronym HATS - Happiness, Adoption, Task Success, and System Usability.
keyPoints: Happiness: Measures user satisfaction and perceived ease of use through surveys like the System Usability Scale (SUS).
Adoption: Tracks the percentage of users who have started using a new feature or product.
Task Success: Quantifies effectiveness (e.g., completion rate) and efficiency (e.g., time on task) of user actions.
System Usability: A combination of metrics that reflect the overall quality of the user experience, often including the above.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on quantitative data (like completion rates) while ignoring qualitative feedback (like user comments).
Measuring system usability with a single metric instead of a balanced scorecard combining multiple HATS measures.
Not connecting UX metrics to specific business goals, making it difficult to prove the value of UX improvements.
